rows.criterion: Single insert into a brand-new/empty tree bootstraps a root
Re-inserting an existing key updates fileID in place (upsert), root unchanged
Inserting keys beyond a node's capacity triggers a correct leaf split
rows.covered_by: TestInsertLeafSplit (250 sequential inserts, real NodeSize=4096, asserts root promoted to internal)

rows.criterion: Median promoted to parent, keys partitioned correctly (leaf split: separator duplicated)
rows.covered_by: splitLeaf/chooseLeafSplit implementation + assertStructuralInvariants (sorted keys, correct fanout) in TestInsertLeafSplit/TestInsertInternalSplit/TestInsertOutOfOrder

rows.criterion: Internal-node split (median promoted alone, not duplicated) when an internal node overflows
rows.covered_by: TestInsertInternalSplit (2000 sequential inserts, forces multi-level splitting)

rows.criterion: Tree remains balanced; correct fanout (structural invariants: sorted keys, len(Children)==len(Keys)+1)
All previously inserted keys remain lookup-able after splits (via real Lookup, not scaffolding)
rows.covered_by: assertAllLookupable in TestInsertLeafSplit/TestInsertInternalSplit/TestInsertOutOfOrder; never-inserted keys correctly report not-found

rows.criterion: Insert order independence (keys arriving out of sorted order still produce a correct tree)
Closes 1.2.2's flagged gap: internal nodes with >= 2 separator keys exercised by Lookup
rows.covered_by: TestInsertInternalSplit explicitly asserts an internal node with len(Keys) >= 2 exists, then runs full-tree Lookup against it

rows.criterion: No regression in 1.2.1 (node serialization) or 1.2.2 (point lookup)
rows.covered_by: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -v: TestNodeSerialization and TestLookup both still pass unchanged
